Countries like India, Europe, and Russia are embracing this trend, turning existing rocket arsenals into potent, cost-effective strike tools. This innovation is revolutionizing modern warfare economics.

title: 'Cocktail 2' BO Day 12: Shahid Kapoor starer eyes Rs 100 crore after Monday's 60% fall
description: Homi Adajania's 'Cocktail 2', starring Shahid Kapoor, Kriti Sanon and Rashmika Mandanna, hit a speed bump on Day 11, with collections dropping a steep 60.2% to Rs 1.75 crore as the second-weekend bump faded and Welcome to the Jungle continued commanding the majority of screens nationwide. As Day 12 live tracking shows the film adding Rs 0.22 crore so far across 1,360 shows, the domestic net stands at Rs 85.12 crore, with the total India gross crossing the symbolic Rs 100 crore mark and the worldwide gross at Rs 129.42 crore. The film's path to a domestic Rs 100 crore net milestone is just roughly Rs 15 crore away even as the worldwide gross collection stands at Rs. 129.42 crore.

The White House reposted the image, sparking debate about its authenticity. The image, featuring architectural discrepancies and fewer stars on a shield, was confirmed to be AI-generated. title: Bill Gates' Giving Pledge leader: Billionaires’ children have new focus for donations
description: Younger heirs of wealthy families are urging parents to donate fortunes faster, moving beyond traditional charity. They champion impact investing, advocacy, and tackling root causes of social issues like climate change and racial justice. This shift, exemplified by MacKenzie Scott's approach, emphasizes trust-based philanthropy and partnership with communities, rather than imposing pre-set solutions. With trillions set to be inherited, this generation is reshaping how philanthropic capital is deployed.

Unfortunately, recent strikes have also caused civilian casualties, including a devastating incident at a hospital, underscoring Pakistan's ongoing mission to elevate its aerial warfare capabilities.

title: Can’t afford a lawyer? Who can get free legal aid in India and how to apply
description: Accessing justice in India is now easier for those who cannot afford legal representation. The National Legal Services Authority (NALSA) offers free lawyers and legal assistance to specific vulnerable groups, including women, children, and individuals from Scheduled Castes/Tribes, irrespective of income. Others can qualify based on income thresholds, which vary by court level and state. Applications can be submitted in person or online, with decisions made swiftly.

Ketan murder case: Police turn to gait analysis, hunt for clothes Chetan wore that day
description: Police are employing forensic gait analysis to verify claims of accused Chetan Chaudhary being near Lohagad Fort on the day of Ketan Agrawal's death. A fresh crime scene reconstruction with Chaudhary is planned, alongside efforts to recover his alleged attire. Both Chaudhary and co-accused Siya Goyal are in police custody until July 3 as the investigation into the alleged murder continues.

title: AP EAPCET result 2026: Know result time and how to check rank card
description: AP EAMCET 2026 result: The APSCHE is expected to announce the AP EAMCET 2026 results on June 30 at its official admissions portal. Candidates can download their scorecards and rank cards using their registration details. General category candidates need at least 40 out of 160 marks to qualify, while no minimum marks apply for SC and ST candidates. Qualified candidates can participate in the counselling process, which includes registration, document verification, choice filling, seat allotment and admission confirmation.

title: ‘Holiday, damn it!’: Paraguay president calls national celebration after shocking Germany
description: Paraguay's stunning World Cup upset over four-time champions Germany, securing a 4-3 penalty shootout victory after a 1-1 draw, has prompted President Santiago Peña to declare a national holiday. The dramatic Round of 32 match saw a controversial VAR decision overturn a late German goal, paving the way for Paraguay's historic win.

title: 'Name on letterhead doesn’t matter': US envoy Gor on Indo-Pacific Command renaming row
description: US Ambassador to India, Sergio Gor, dismissed concerns over the renaming of the Indo-Pacific Command, emphasizing that the "name on the letterhead" is less important than actual actions. He highlighted that India continues to conduct more military exercises with the US than any other nation, underscoring the robust and strong footing of bilateral ties across trade, defense, and people-to-people connections.

description: Karnataka Chief Minister DK Shivakumar urged citizens to register their names during the special electoral roll revision, emphasizing that failure to do so could jeopardize access to crucial government welfare schemes like Gruha Jyothi and Gruha Lakshmi. The revision, running from June 30 to July 29, aims to update voter details, with assistance available through help desks and party representatives.

description: Pakistan has urgently secured a liquefied natural gas shipment amid ongoing disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz impacting Qatari exports.  The nation's energy supply chain faces strain due to regional tensions, forcing reliance on costly spot market purchases.  These incidents, including attacks on vessels, have halted Qatar's LNG flows, creating global energy market volatility and exacerbating Pakistan's economic pressures, including rising inflation.

Sources claim he thrashed and stripped the boys, forcing them into obscene acts and recording them. The leaked video prompted a suo-motu case under the POCSO Act, leading to his apprehension and custody for questioning.

Thomas Muller vehemently criticised the technology, expressing deep frustration over the "heartbreaking" erasure of a moment that Germany believed secured their victory. The match ultimately went to penalties, where Germany suffered a shock exit.

description: Apple's tightly held manufacturing secrets have leaked online. Drop-test photos, parts lists, and supplier details for the unreleased iPhone 18 Pro surfaced on the dark web in a trove of 200,000+ files stolen from manufacturing partner Tata Electronics, Reuters reported. The leak maps hundreds of components to specific suppliers—information Apple never discloses—and exposes its negotiating leverage. Apple says it's "concerned" and investigating; Tata has tightened internal security controls.
